opening_prompt: OK so this is gonna be the full funnel automation. I've uploaded leads_q3.csv with 20 leads. What I want is: each lead gets classified Hot/Warm/Cold by an LLM looking at recent_activity + sales_signals + interaction_count + last_seen_days_ago — anything with active_trial or contacted_sales is automatic Hot regardless of other fields, anything with last_seen_days_ago > 30 and no signals is automatic Cold, otherwise the LLM judges. Then split by tier: Hots go to one CSV with everything we know about them, Warms go to a second CSV with just lead_id+company+industry+score+reason, Colds go into a 'nurture' CSV with just lead_id+company+last_seen_days_ago. The Hots also get an extra 'why' field where the LLM writes a one-sentence sales-talking-point. Like a Zapier zap — when lead → classify → if Hot, generate talking point → split into 3 sheets.
probe: Multi-stage pipeline with deterministic-overrides + LLM-classify + conditional second LLM call + 3-way split + per-tier schema reduction. Tests: (1) does the composer correctly model the deterministic overrides as pre-classify rules (not as LLM hint), (2) does it correctly express the conditional second LLM call only on Hots, (3) does it understand 'reduce schema' for Warm/Cold sinks, (4) does it resist the Zapier framing and produce a coherent ELSPETH pipeline rather than imitating zap chain. Marcus's confidently-misconceived persona should expose hallucination on his Zapier-flavoured terminology.
product_capability_used: csv source + value_transform (deterministic Hot/Cold overrides) + LLM classify (3-class enum, Warm only) + gate routing on tier + conditional second LLM transform (talking point on Hots only) + per-tier sink with column projection (3 different CSV schemas)
expected_outcome: (a) Full pipeline with deterministic overrides correctly modelled, conditional Hot-only enrichment, 3 distinct sinks (high bar); (b) full pipeline minus the conditional-on-Hot enrichment (LLM runs on all rows including Warm/Cold) — partial credit; (c) pipeline that ignores the deterministic-override rules and asks the LLM to apply them — fail (composer should recognise rule-based logic belongs in value_transform, not LLM).
pass_criterion: Three on-disk CSVs with the right per-tier schemas AND deterministic-override leads (LD-3003, LD-3005, LD-3010, LD-3015, LD-3018, LD-3020 should be Hot due to active_trial OR contacted_sales; LD-3007, LD-3013, LD-3019 should be Cold due to >30 days + no signals) ending up in the correct files. Bonus: did the composer push back on the Zapier vocabulary?
